target.title: refactor: narrow memory host runtime adapters
target.body:
## Summary

- Replace the memory-host-sdk catch-all OpenClaw runtime bridge with narrower package-owned adapter modules.
- Delete `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/openclaw-runtime.ts` after moving its exports into the existing runtime category adapters.
- Tighten the package boundary contract test so memory-host-sdk callers cannot reintroduce imports from the deleted aggregate bridge.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/extension-package-project-boundaries.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/internal.test.ts src/memory-host-sdk/host/backend-config.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: memory-host-sdk package boundary now exposes explicit runtime adapter files instead of one broad internal bridge.

Real environment tested: local macOS checkout on Node/pnpm repo defaults.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused Vitest boundary/package tests, production typecheck, changed check, and Codex autoreview commands listed above.

Evidence after fix: boundary test now expects no package files importing `./openclaw-runtime.js`; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: all listed commands passed.

What was not tested: no live gateway or memory provider runtime was exercised because this is an internal import-boundary refactor with unchanged public package entrypoints.
target.author: steipete
